headline: A US government export-control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominates this cycle, alongside a race toward 1,000-plus tokens per second inference.
body: The recommended channels converge on the abrupt shutdown of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5: Better Stack reports the export-control directive requiring immediate disablement for all customers [4], Cole Medin ties the pull to jailbreak concerns and compares it to Claude Opus 4.8 [1], Cyril Imhof frames it as an EU-access regulatory question [3], and IBM Technology debates the model's tier routing alongside Apple's WWDC 2026 shift toward NVIDIA cloud compute [6]. A parallel thread is the inference speed race: Better Stack covers Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed hitting over 1,000 tokens per second via FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime [0], and Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma reaching the same throughput on H100s by moving inference from memory-bound to compute-bound through bidirectional diffusion passes [2]. Julia Turc's interview with Groq founder Jonathan Ross details the LPU architecture, static scheduling, cost-per-token Pareto curves, and an NVIDIA agreement [8], while Better Stack also profiles CopilotKit for agentic generative UI in React with human-in-the-loop approval [5]. Non-recommended channels echo the Fable 5 ban with sharper commentary [11][12] and roll up open-source releases like GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, and MiniMax M3 [10].
